title: Vi Vision Legibility (meaning-fields)
description:
The LEGIBILITY meaning-fields — how Tim (a non-developer who reads by MEANING) sees what a thing IS, resolved from the thing's own row, never from a machine name.

The operator-legibility law (THE_FACTORY.md):
- name = THIS thing's human name (never a jargon title, never a glyph, never a filename leaf).
- is   = what KIND of thing it is, in plain words ('a note saved', 'a role's voice').
- Every surface renders the SAME name + is (one voice across the gallery face, the inspector, the centre chip). Meaning-words LEAD; glyphs are decoration after, never instead.
- name resolves PER-INSTANCE: declared name -> a derived human name (humanise the leaf: strip extension, de-snake/camel, title-case) -> the raw leaf only as last-resort floor.

Design intent:
- This is a REUSABLE field-set carried on any addressable type (a decision, a component, a corpus unit). It is the shape RUNG-C renders through and the shape the decision-card reads its labels from.
- name + is are REQUIRED on every type that carries legibility (validateLegibility fails loud at birth if missing); fills + why are optional enrichment.

Relationships:
- runtime/legibility.js (SEED_LEGIBILITY_FIELDS, resolveLegibility) is the resolver; this schema is its contract.
- Referenced by decision.schema.json (and intended for every addressable type).

name.description: THIS thing's HUMAN name — what Tim calls it. Never a machine id, jargon title, or glyph. Rendered as the anchor wherever the thing is referenced.

is.description: What KIND of thing it is, in plain words (the 'what is this' one-liner). Distinct from name: name = which one, is = what kind. Never the anchor.

fills.description: Optional. What fills it / what it accepts (its kind or accepts), in plain words.

why.description: Optional. Why you'd look at it / what it's for.
